tasks.title: Hello world utility
tasks.spec:
Write a Python function called `greet(name: str) -> str` that returns f"Ahoj, {name}!".

Output requirements:
- Output ONLY the Python code in a single fenced ``` block.
- No prose explanation before or after.
- The code must include a one-line docstring.

tasks.title: CZK currency formatter
tasks.spec:
Write a Python function `czk_format(amount: float) -> str` that formats a number as Czech crowns.

Czech currency formatting rules:
- Thousands separator: SPACE (not comma, not dot, not narrow no-break space — regular space)
- Decimal separator: COMMA
- Always 2 decimal places
- Suffix: " Kč" (space before "Kč")
- Negative numbers: minus sign before the digits

Examples (these MUST all pass):
    czk_format(1234.5)      == "1 234,50 Kč"
    czk_format(1000000)     == "1 000 000,00 Kč"
    czk_format(0)           == "0,00 Kč"
    czk_format(-99.9)       == "-99,90 Kč"
    czk_format(7)           == "7,00 Kč"
    czk_format(123.456)     == "123,46 Kč"   # rounds half-to-even or normal rounding both fine
    czk_format(999999.99)   == "999 999,99 Kč"

Output requirements:
- Output ONLY a single fenced ```python code block.
- The block must contain just the function definition (and any imports it needs at the top).
- No prose, no test cases, no explanation outside the code block.
- The function must be self-contained — no external libraries beyond the Python stdlib.

tasks.title: CZK currency formatter (Gemini retry, no questions)

DO NOT propose a plan.
DO NOT mention what you are about to do.

Just write Python code RIGHT NOW.

Czech currency formatting rules:
- Thousands separator: REGULAR SPACE

    czk_format(1000000)     == "1 000 000,00 Kč"

    czk_format(-99.9)       == "-99,90 Kč"

Output format — strictly:

\`\`\`python

    ...
\`\`\`

NOTHING before the opening fence. NOTHING after the closing fence.
